IMF Declares "Little Room to Cut Interest Rates in 2026" | NO Rate Cuts Expected | HIGH Confidence**

The International Monetary Fund's Executive Board concluded its 2026 Article IV Consultation with the United States on April 2, 2026, explicitly stating there is "little room to cut interest rates in 2026."[1] The IMF projects the fed funds rate will move only from 3.6% to 3.4% — essentially one c
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: **Core PCE inflation remains stuck at 2
signals.raw: **Core PCE inflation remains stuck at 2.9%** (2025), with projections showing 2.6% by end of 2026 — well above the 2% target.[1]

signals.headline: **Tariffs are sustaining inflation:** Goods prices pushed higher by tariffs while services inflation cooled, netting sid
signals.raw: **Tariffs are sustaining inflation:** Goods prices pushed higher by tariffs while services inflation cooled, netting sideways movement.[1] Powell indicated goods inflation should peak around Q1 2026 assuming no additional tariff rounds.[2]

signals.headline: **Labor market weakness:** Powell admitted the Fed believes official payroll figures overstate job growth by roughly 60,
signals.raw: **Labor market weakness:** Powell admitted the Fed believes official payroll figures overstate job growth by roughly 60,000 per month, with actual job creation potentially negative.[2]

signals.headline: **GDP growth modest:** IMF projects 2
signals.raw:
**GDP growth modest:** IMF projects 2.4% growth in 2026, but this peaks this year and decays toward 1.8%.[1]

This IMF statement significantly reduces probability of rate cuts in 2026 prediction markets.
